name: Brand Guidelines
description: Visual brand tokens for all motion graphics and infographics — colours, font, animation style, Remotion project location
type: user
---

Always apply these brand elements automatically to any motion graphic or infographic unless explicitly overridden.

## Colours
- Background: radial gradient `#e0e1dd` (centre) → `#6b6b6b` (mid) → `#222222` (edge)
- Primary ring / accent: `#415a77` (steel blue)
- Secondary ring / accent: `#c2a46d` (warm gold)
- Text primary: `#1D1D1F`
- Text secondary: `rgba(29,29,31,0.45)`
- Text muted: `rgba(29,29,31,0.6)`
- Text accent: `#415a77`

## Typography
- Font: Montserrat Extra Bold (weight 800) — all display text, no exceptions

## Animation (Remotion)
- Duration: 3 seconds for callout/stat graphics
- In: spring scale 0.84→1 + staggered left-to-right reveals
- Out: Gaussian blur dissolve (blur 0→24px + opacity 1→0, 0.8s, starts at 2.2s)
- Springs: snappy `{damping:15, stiffness:220}` / bouncy `{damping:12, stiffness:280}` / smooth `{damping:14, stiffness:200}`

## Brand Token File
All tokens in `C:\Users\mcopp\focus-remotion\src\brand.ts` — always import from there for new compositions, never hardcode values.

**Why:** User said time is their biggest asset — brand consistency must be automatic, not something they fiddle with per project.

message.content: Great you now have access to my previous work. Now that I have made a couple of videos I would like to run a follower campaign on Facebook. I need a simple ad creative that intices people to follow and/or like my page. Is this something you can help me do while following brand guidelines etc? I have not done this with you before so I am not sure if this is something you can help me with. I need it to be designed so that it appears in peoples posts. I need it to have a clear title and onscreen text. I am not sure what those should be.

content.text:
Yes, this is exactly what I can help with — a Remotion animated ad that renders to a video file you upload directly to Facebook Ads Manager as a feed video ad.

**A few decisions before I build:**

**1. Format**
Facebook feed ads perform best at **1:1 (1080×1080)** or **4:5 (1080×1350)**. I'd recommend 4:5 — takes up more screen real estate in feed. This means a new composition size, not the standard 1920×1080.

**2. Duration**
For a follower campaign, **6–8 seconds** is the sweet spot — long enough to land a message, short enough to hold attention without a skip.

**3. Copy — based on your content niche**

Looking at your scripts, your audience is business owners who produce (or want to produce) video content. Here are three directions:

| Option | Headline | Sub-copy | CTA |
|---|---|---|---|
| A | **Stop wasting time on video.** | Weekly systems for business owners who want to look pro without the production drag. | Follow for weekly tips |
| B | **Your video doesn't need to be perfect.** | I share the frameworks that help business owners post confidently and consistently. | Follow to keep up |
| C | **One take. Real authority.** | Video tips for business owners who'd rather grow than edit. | Follow for weekly insights |

Option C ties directly to your "1 Take Mental Model" positioning and is the most distinctive.

**4. What I'd build**
- Brand background (radial gradient)
- Animated headline springs in
- Sub-copy fades in below
- Your page name / handle at the bottom
- "Follow" CTA button element
- Out: blur dissolve

**Questions before I start:**
1. Which copy direction (A/B/C), or do you want to adjust any of the wording?
2. What is your Facebook page name/handle to include on the creative?
3. Do you want 4:5 or 1:1?

message.content: I am not sure which copy works best, I like C. It is more punchy and to the point. I like section 4.  My page name is Michael Coppard - Video Stratagist. I am not sure if 4:5 or 1:1 will convert better.

content.text:
Quick note — you wrote "Stratagist" — I'll use **"Video Strategist"** (correct spelling) unless that's intentional branding.

On 4:5 vs 1:1: build 4:5, run it, check performance after a week, then A/B test 1:1 if needed. You can always re-render at 1:1 later.

Let me check brand.ts so I use the exact exports correctly.

**`FacebookFollowerAd.tsx`** — 6 seconds, 1080×1350 (4:5)

| Element | Detail |
|---|---|
| Background | Brand radial gradient |
| Headline | "One take. Real authority." — 108px, Extra Bold |
| Divider | Gold bar, scales in from centre |
| Sub-copy | "Video tips for business owners who'd rather grow than edit." — muted |
| CTA pill | Steel blue rounded button — "Follow for weekly insights" |
| Byline | MICHAEL COPPARD — VIDEO STRATEGIST — small caps |
| Out | Blur dissolve from 5.2s |

**Render command:**
```
npm run render:prores:fb-follower
```

No `--scale 2` on this one — 1080×1350 is native Facebook feed resolution and 4K would be overkill for social ads.

**Before you run it** — open Remotion Studio and preview `FacebookFollowerAd` to check the layout feels right, then render. Let me know if you want any copy, sizing, or timing tweaks.

content.text:
Done. Render it with:

```
cd focus-remotion
npm run render:png:fb-follower
```

Outputs `out/FacebookFollowerAdStatic.png` at 1080×1350 — ready to upload directly to Facebook Ads Manager as an image ad. No `--scale 2` needed since 1080px is native resolution for this format.

message.content: Can we create another static image, this time take away the vingiette, and lets ask ourselves, what is the incentive for people to follow my page, what are they going to get out of it?

content.text:
Good question to stop and answer before building. Let me think through the value proposition based on your content.

**What does someone get from following you?**

Looking at your 13 scripts, your content gives business owners:
- One actionable video tip per week — specific, not vague
- Systems: lighting, audio, delivery, batching, positioning — the full stack
- A framework to record in one take and look credible without a production team
- Authority-building through content, not just vanity metrics

**Proposed copy direction — benefit-led layout:**

```
Every week I share one video tip
for business owners who want to look
credible on camera — without the crew,
the cost, or the retakes.

Follow to get weekly tips on:
  → Lighting & audio that builds trust
  → One-take delivery systems
  → Video that attracts clients
  → Authority without the production budget
```

Or shorter and punchier:

```
Follow and every week you get:

One tip. One framework. One less hour
wasted on video that doesn't work.

Lighting. Audio. Delivery. Systems.
Everything a business owner needs
```
